title: Refactor TauriToolContext constructors to builder pattern + McpConfig struct
analysis:
TauriToolContext has 4 constructors (new, new_with_selection, with_event_emitter, with_event_emitter_and_selection) for a 2^2 combinatorial explosion over 2 optional fields (event_emitter, selection). The builder pattern collapses this to a single entry point. Additionally, claude_bridge.rs::new_inner takes a 3-tuple Option<(Arc<Mutex<EngineSession>>, F, Arc<RwLock<SelectionInfo>>)> requiring #[allow(clippy::type_complexity)], which should become a named McpConfig<F> struct.

Call sites:
- main.rs:263 — uses with_event_emitter_and_selection (both optional fields)
- claude_bridge.rs:268 — uses new_with_selection (selection only)
- test helpers use new() (no optionals) and new_with_selection / with_event_emitter

The with_event_emitter() constructor creates an unshared Arc<RwLock<SelectionInfo>> — a footgun since selection is never updated by the frontend. After this refactor, the builder makes the two optional dimensions explicit and independent.

claude_bridge_tests.rs does NOT reference TauriToolContext or the mcp_config tuple — it only tests SidecarHandle::from_parts (no MCP). So it does not need modification.

Approach: (1) Add McpConfig struct + builder on mcp_context.rs alongside existing constructors. (2) Write tests for builder. (3) Migrate call sites one by one. (4) Remove old constructors + type_complexity suppression.

steps.description: Write test `builder_with_no_options_matches_new` — construct via TauriToolContext::builder(engine).build() and assert get_selection returns empty, focus_entity succeeds without emitter. This validates the builder with defaults.

Add TauriToolContextBuilder struct and TauriToolContext::builder(engine) -> TauriToolContextBuilder, with build() method that creates TauriToolContext with None event_emitter and fresh empty SelectionInfo Arc. Keep old constructors intact for now.

Write test `builder_with_selection_matches_new_with_selection` — construct via builder(engine).with_selection(arc).build() and assert get_selection returns the pre-set selected_entity from the shared Arc.
Add with_selection(Arc<RwLock<SelectionInfo>>) method to TauriToolContextBuilder, storing it as Option and using it in build() if present.

Write test `builder_with_event_emitter_records_events` — construct via builder(engine).with_event_emitter(closure).build() and assert focus_entity triggers the emitter callback. Also write `builder_with_both_options` test using both with_event_emitter and with_selection.
Add with_event_emitter(impl Fn) method to TauriToolContextBuilder. The build() method boxes the closure into EventEmitter if provided. Both optional fields now work through the builder.

Write test `mcp_config_struct_stores_fields` — construct McpConfig { engine, event_sink, selection } and assert all three fields are accessible. This validates the named struct compiles and holds the right types.

Add pub struct McpConfig<F> { pub engine, pub event_sink: F, pub selection } in claude_bridge.rs. Replace the 3-tuple in new_inner's mcp_config parameter with Option<McpConfig<F>>. Update from_parts_with_mcp to construct McpConfig and pass it. Remove #[allow(clippy::type_complexity)] from new_inner. Update destructuring inside new_inner's closure (ref engine, ref sink, ref selection → named field access).
Migrate main.rs call site: replace TauriToolContext::with_event_emitter_and_selection(...) with TauriToolContext::builder(engine).with_event_emitter(closure).with_selection(arc).build().
Migrate claude_bridge.rs call site: replace TauriToolContext::new_with_selection(engine, selection) with TauriToolContext::builder(engine).with_selection(selection).build().

Migrate test helpers: update make_tauri_context() in mcp_context_tests.rs and mcp_dispatch_tests.rs to use builder(engine).build(). Update all TauriToolContext::new_with_selection calls to builder(engine).with_selection(s).build(). Update TauriToolContext::with_event_emitter calls to builder(engine).with_event_emitter(f).build().

Remove all 4 old constructors (new, new_with_selection, with_event_emitter, with_event_emitter_and_selection) from TauriToolContext. Verify no remaining references to the old constructors. Run cargo check / cargo test to confirm everything compiles and passes.

design_decisions.decision: Builder struct as separate type TauriToolContextBuilder rather than methods on TauriToolContext itself
design_decisions.rationale: A dedicated builder struct allows the with_event_emitter method to accept impl Fn (generics) while TauriToolContext stores the erased Box<dyn Fn>. The builder does the boxing in build(), keeping the type-erased storage internal. This also follows Rust idiom (e.g., tokio::runtime::Builder, reqwest::ClientBuilder).

design_decisions.decision: Builder defaults to fresh empty Arc<RwLock<SelectionInfo>> (matching old `new()` behavior)
design_decisions.rationale: Preserves backward compatibility: callers that don't care about selection get the same behavior as before. The fresh Arc is explicitly documented as unshared (not connected to frontend), making the footgun visible rather than hidden.

design_decisions.decision: McpConfig<F> as a named generic struct rather than a non-generic struct with trait object
design_decisions.rationale: The event_sink must be generic (F: Fn(String, Value)) because it's used inside a non-async closure in the reader task. A trait object would work but adds unnecessary indirection. Keeping it generic matches the existing pattern and eliminates the type_complexity lint naturally.

These tests only use SidecarHandle::from_parts (no MCP config) and never reference TauriToolContext. The McpConfig struct change is internal to new_inner, which from_parts calls with None — no test surface changes needed.

design_decisions.decision: Migrate all call sites before removing old constructors (steps 9-11 before step 12)
design_decisions.rationale: Incremental migration ensures each step compiles. The old constructors coexist with the builder during migration, allowing the diff at each step to be small and reviewable.

reuse.what: EventEmitter type alias (Box<dyn Fn(&str, serde_json::Value) + Send + Sync>)
reuse.how: Reuse this existing type alias in the builder's build() method for boxing the closure
The builder's build() method uses the same Arc::new(RwLock::new(SelectionInfo { selected_entity: None, hovered_entity: None })) as the default when no selection is provided
New builder tests reuse these helpers unchanged — only the TauriToolContext construction call changes
